---
title: "Media gallery"
description: "A per-venue library for every photo you use across your menu, item detail pages, and landing page."
sidebarTitle: "Gallery"
icon: "images"
---

The gallery is one place for every image your venue uses on Rumizi. Upload
once and use a photo on as many items, categories, or the landing hero as
you want. No re-uploading, no duplicates.

## Where to find it

In your workspace, go to **Gallery**. Direct URL:
`rumizi.com/your-venue/w/gallery`.

## Uploading

<Steps>
  <Step title="Drag or pick files">
    You can upload one at a time or drop a batch in. Rumizi uploads in
    parallel so a big batch is fast.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Automatic optimization">
    Every upload is processed automatically. See
    [Image optimization](#image-optimization) for what happens.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Use it">
    Tap **Use for item** or **Use for category** on any gallery image to
    copy it into a menu item, category, or landing page slot.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What you can upload

- **JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF** — standard formats, no conversion needed.
- **HEIC / HEIF** — converted to WebP automatically (iPhone photos).
- **Video** — short clips work for the landing page hero.

## Image optimization

Every upload is processed so your menu stays fast on mobile data:

- **Resize** to max **2048px** on the long edge (never upscaled).
- **Convert** to WebP at quality 80.
- **Strip** EXIF metadata and auto-rotate.
- **Skip** already-optimized WebP files under 100 KB.

A 5 MB phone photo typically ends up under 300 KB without any visible
quality loss.

## Crawl from a URL

If your dishes already have good photos on your own website, Rumizi can
crawl the page and pull them into the gallery. Paste the URL on the
gallery page, pick the ones you want, and import. Useful when migrating
from another menu platform.

## Bookmarklet

For one-off grabs, you can drag the Rumizi bookmarklet into your browser
bookmarks bar. While looking at a page with a photo you like, tap the
bookmarklet — the images get pushed into your gallery. Safety limits keep
the bookmarklet from pulling thousands of images at once.

## Tags on media

Each image can have tags. Filter by tag to find what you need quickly
when your gallery grows past 50+ items.

## Deleting

Deleting an image removes it from the gallery but does **not** rip it out
of the items you've already applied it to — those items keep a copy. If
you want to swap an item photo, update the item instead of deleting from
the gallery.
